question: The historiographical concept of 'history from below' was applied to the Caribbean most forcefully by
options: Navigating economic dependence, climate vulnerability, geopolitical pressure and the legacy of colonialism simultaneously
It represents one of the earliest modern examples of state-sanctioned ethnic cleansing in the region
Sparked international calls for reparations, as estimates suggested it cost Haiti the equivalent of billions in modern currency
Richard Hart, CLR James and later Sidney Mintz, centering enslaved and working-class experiences
answer: Richard Hart, CLR James and later Sidney Mintz, centering enslaved and working-class experiences

question: The Haitian intellectual Anténor Firmin's 'On the Equality of the Human Races' (1885) was
options: Anti-imperialism, Pan-Africanism, critique of Babylon (the Western state system) and repatriation theology
The African Diaspora's resistance to European capitalism, with the Caribbean as a central site
The central role of women in reproduction, resistance and post-emancipation community-building, largely erased from official histories
A direct rebuttal of scientific racism, arguing for universal human equality before such ideas were mainstream
answer: A direct rebuttal of scientific racism, arguing for universal human equality before such ideas were mainstream

question: The concept of 'plantation slavery as primitive accumulation' (Eric Williams thesis, extended by Cedric Robinson) argues
options: The African Diaspora's resistance to European capitalism, with the Caribbean as a central site
That Caribbean slavery was the original site of capital accumulation that financed Western modernity
The central role of women in reproduction, resistance and post-emancipation community-building, largely erased from official histories
Navigating economic dependence, climate vulnerability, geopolitical pressure and the legacy of colonialism simultaneously
answer: That Caribbean slavery was the original site of capital accumulation that financed Western modernity

question: The Rastafari intellectual tradition's contribution to Caribbean political thought includes
options: The central role of women in reproduction, resistance and post-emancipation community-building, largely erased from official histories
'Industrialisation by Invitation' (Arthur Lewis model) — attracting foreign capital rather than developing local industry
A direct rebuttal of scientific racism, arguing for universal human equality before such ideas were mainstream
Anti-imperialism, Pan-Africanism, critique of Babylon (the Western state system) and repatriation theology
answer: Anti-imperialism, Pan-Africanism, critique of Babylon (the Western state system) and repatriation theology

question: The 'Grenada Revolution's' New Jewel Movement was influenced theoretically by
options: Caribbean dependency theory, Black Power philosophy and Cuban revolutionary socialism
The African Diaspora's resistance to European capitalism, with the Caribbean as a central site
The central role of women in reproduction, resistance and post-emancipation community-building, largely erased from official histories
A direct rebuttal of scientific racism, arguing for universal human equality before such ideas were mainstream
answer: Caribbean dependency theory, Black Power philosophy and Cuban revolutionary socialism

question: The historical significance of the Haitian indemnity debt finally being acknowledged by France in 2022
options: Sparked international calls for reparations, as estimates suggested it cost Haiti the equivalent of billions in modern currency
Haitian refugees fleeing the revolution settled in New Orleans, fundamentally shaping its culture
The central role of women in reproduction, resistance and post-emancipation community-building, largely erased from official histories
Richard Hart, CLR James and later Sidney Mintz, centering enslaved and working-class experiences
answer: Sparked international calls for reparations, as estimates suggested it cost Haiti the equivalent of billions in modern currency

question: The post-independence Caribbean political economy followed a model known as
options: A deepening regional integration ambition, moving from free trade to a common market and eventually a single economy
That Caribbean slavery was the original site of capital accumulation that financed Western modernity
Colonial power structures persist through race and knowledge systems long after formal independence
'Industrialisation by Invitation' (Arthur Lewis model) — attracting foreign capital rather than developing local industry
answer: 'Industrialisation by Invitation' (Arthur Lewis model) — attracting foreign capital rather than developing local industry

question: The Caribbean feminist historiography has highlighted
options: The central role of women in reproduction, resistance and post-emancipation community-building, largely erased from official histories

A direct rebuttal of scientific racism, arguing for universal human equality before such ideas were mainstream
Navigating economic dependence, climate vulnerability, geopolitical pressure and the legacy of colonialism simultaneously
answer: The central role of women in reproduction, resistance and post-emancipation community-building, largely erased from official histories

options: Haitian refugees fleeing the revolution settled in New Orleans, fundamentally shaping its culture
Caribbean dependency theory, Black Power philosophy and Cuban revolutionary socialism
That Caribbean slavery was the original site of capital accumulation that financed Western modernity
It represents one of the earliest modern examples of state-sanctioned ethnic cleansing in the region

'Industrialisation by Invitation' (Arthur Lewis model) — attracting foreign capital rather than developing local industry
